item.title: Fintech SumUp Lines Up Banks for Possible London IPO
item.description: SumUp Payments Ltd. is lining up investment banks to arrange a potential initial public offering in London, according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be one of the biggest listings the UK capital has seen in recent years.

item.title: Tory Burch Seeks Loan to Buy Back General Atlantic’s Stake
item.description: Luxury retailer Tory Burch LLC intends to repurchase private equity firm General Atlantic’s stake with some of the proceeds of a planned $700 million leveraged loan.

item.title: BOE’s Bailey Urges Regulators to Assess AI Cyber Risk to Banks
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said global regulators need to rapidly evaluate the threat posed by Anthropic’s Mythos artificial intelligence model.

item.title: Canada Regulator Reviewing Banks’ Private-Credit Exposures
item.description: Canada’s bank regulator is examining lenders’ exposure to hedge funds and private-credit shops — as well as how they transfer risk to those firms — with further inquiries planned.

item.title: US to Let Iran Oil Sanctions Waiver Expire Amid Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The Trump administration will allow a waiver temporarily authorizing the purchase of certain Iranian crude oil to expire this weekend, according to a White House official.

item.title: Lithuania Pension Reform Triggers Mass Exodus as Savers Cash Out
item.description: Lithuania’s decision to allow early access to retirement cash resulted in about half a million of 1.4 million people opting out of a pension-saving scheme in the first quarter, suggesting a potential spending spree ahead.

item.title: Saks Offloads Its Gulfstream Jet in Push to Shrink in Bankruptcy
item.description: Saks Global Enterprises won court approval to sell its Gulfstream jet that executives used for business and personal travel, as the bankrupt luxury retailer slashes debt and operating expenses.

item.title: Sotheby’s to Sell $825 Million Junk Bond In Refinancing Push
item.description: Sotheby’s is seizing a window of opportunity to refinance debt due next year, before any disruptions from the US-Iran negotiations can derail capital-raising efforts.

item.title: CSN Mining Unit in Talks for $200 Million Iron Ore Prepay Deal
item.description: Brazilian miner CSN Mineracao SA is sounding out traders for a roughly $200 million prepayment against iron ore cargoes, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Gasoline, Diesel Pump Prices Reach All-Time Seasonal Highs
item.description: Retail gasoline and diesel prices in the US are at their highest seasonal levels ever, a pain point for consumers looking ahead to summer travel.

item.title: Microsoft Takes Over Stargate Data Center From OpenAI in Norway
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has agreed to rent data center capacity at a site in Norway that was initially intended for OpenAI and marketed as part of the artificial intelligence company’s Stargate initiative.

item.title: The Entire Iran War Selloff Has Been Erased
item.description: Another v-shaped bounce

item.title: Dow, Exxon and Rivals Are Raising Plastic Prices as Iran War Convulses Oil Market
item.description: Dow Inc. and Exxon Mobil Corp. are among the companies boosting prices for plastics as the sector grapples with supply shocks from the US-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Alternative Data Increasingly Helping Economists Gauge War Shock
item.description: Economists expect their reliance on alternative data to increase in the next year, citing a need for higher-frequency indicators amid economic uncertainty tied to the war in Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: BlackRock Brazil Fund Records Largest Daily Inflow Since 2017
item.description: Investors poured money into BlackRock’s exchange-traded fund tracking Brazilian stocks at the fastest pace in almost nine years on Monday, as a resurgence in global risk appetite fuels a surge in the country’s equity market.

item.title: Thames Rescue Deal Stumbles With Ofwat Board Split Over Approval
item.description: The board of UK watchdog Ofwat is split over whether to approve a rescue deal offered by the creditors of Thames Water, increasing the risk the utility could collapse into special administration.

item.title: TD, BMO to Act as Market Makers for New Credit Futures Contract
item.description: TD Securities and BMO Capital Markets are acting as liquidity providers for a new Canadian bank credit futures contract, according to the Montreal Exchange.

item.title: IMF Urges Nations to Stay at Frontier of Mounting AI Risks
item.description: Governments and regulators must “stay at the frontier” of rising threats from artificial intelligence, a top official at the International Monetary Fund warned on Tuesday, as fears about the destructive potential of Anthropic PBC’s new models dominated conversations at the fund’s Spring Meetings.

item.title: Lagarde Welcomes New Hungarian Leader’s Plan to Join Euro
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde welcomed signals from Hungary’s incoming leadership that they would take steps to join the euro zone, which she called the natural path for the European Union’s members.

item.title: Lagarde, Worried About AI, Lauds Anthropic’s Approach on Mythos
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde praised Anthropic PBC for limiting the release of its latest artificial intelligence model, and called for greater safeguards on the technology.

item.title: Telesat’s Rebuild Gets Boost From Canada’s Defense War Chest
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney has opened the spigot for Canada to invest tens of billions of dollars more on defense, which means the country — very belatedly — just hit its North Atlantic Treaty Organization commitment to spend 2% of gross domestic product on security.

item.title: Altman-Backed Startup Raises $170 Million for Robocar Networks
item.description: Glydways Inc., the robocar startup backed by Sam Altman, is in talks to raise an additional $250 million on the heels of a roughly $170 million Series C round, underlining the high hopes and daunting hurdles of developing closed-loop self-driving car systems.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Private Credit Fund to Sell $500 Million Bond
item.description: A Goldman Sachs Group Inc. private credit fund is selling high-grade bonds on Tuesday, according to people with knowledge of the matter, making it the second private credit fund to do so this week.

item.title: Fed Chair Nominee Warsh Discloses Assets Above $190 Million
item.description: Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, has disclosed assets with his wife Jane Lauder that total at least $192 million, though the actual figure for their holdings is certainly much higher.

item.title: Gunvor Founder Törnqvist Got Over $1 Billion Upfront in Buyout
item.description: Gunvor Group’s outgoing owner Torbjörn Törnqvist received over $1 billion in upfront payments as part of a hasty sale of his company to its management, according to documents seen by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Jane Street in Talks to Back Fluidstack at $18 Billion Valuation
item.description: The cloud-computing startup Fluidstack Ltd. is holding funding talks with investors to bring in about $1 billion at a target valuation of $18 billion, according to people briefed on the matter.

item.title: Lagarde Says She Won’t Leave ECB Early With Clouds on Horizon
item.description: President Christine Lagarde said an early exit from the European Central Bank isn’t currently an option for her as the euro-zone economy navigates challenging times.

item.title: Deutsche Bank’s Saravelos Says Sell Dollar as War Risks Peaked
item.description: The risks linked to the war in Iran will be subsiding and it is time to bet on a weaker US dollar, according to Deutsche Bank AG.

item.title: Market ‘Sugar High’ Will Send Stocks to Record, Wells Fargo Says
item.description: The war in Iran may be ongoing, but traders have largely moved on, as a number of bullish catalysts in the coming months are poised to propel the S&P 500 Index to new highs, according to Wells Fargo Securities.

item.title: Mideast Conflict Could Still Trigger Market Turmoil, IMF Warns
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East could yet trigger the kind of turmoil that markets have so far avoided, the International Monetary Fund warned, adding its voice to a rising chorus of concern about the financial stability impact of the war.
